summary: This cluster investigates the potential of red algae species from Jeju Island, Korea, as natural sources of angiotensin-I converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors. Extracts from *Lithophyllum okamurae*, *Lomentaria catenata*, and *Ahnfeltiopsis flabelliformis* demonstrated the strongest in vitro ACE inhibitory activity, with IC50 values as low as 12.21–13.84 µg mL⁻¹ for aqueous extracts. The research systematically screened both methanol and aqueous extracts from twenty-six species, identifying *Grateloupia filicina*, *Sinkoraena lancifolia*, and *Gracilaria vermiculophylla* as additional promising candidates. The geographic focus on Jeju Island highlights the regional biodiversity of marine macroalgae as a source of bioactive compounds for managing hypertension.
entity_sample: treat of essential hypertension and heart failure in human
